commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=376053cebbbc6151da15ecd8b130c1234b541b27)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `collected.length === 0` guard to memory-host-sdk archive cron prompt classification and adds a regression test for a mid-transcript `[cron:...]` user message.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +36. Total +37 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current `buildSessionEntry` can classify a usage-counted archive as cron-generated from user text, clear collected content, and skip later messages before memory sync indexes the entry. I did not run a live end-to-end `memory_search` query in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer judgment on the trusted archive cron-provenance boundary before automation chooses between first-turn human recall and metadata-less cron opacity.

target.title: fix(memory-host-sdk): prevent mid-transcript [cron:...] from wiping archive content
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Issue #98241: When a user types text starting with `[cron:` mid-conversation (e.g., `[cron:daily-digest] why does the archive vanish?`), the archive content classifier matches it as a cron-generated prompt and wipes ALL previously collected content from the archive. This makes earlier conversation history invisible to `memory_search`.

## Why This Change Was Made

The `isGeneratedCronPromptMessage` check at `session-files.ts:843` was intended to detect cron-run transcripts where the very first user message is a cron prompt. However, the regex `DIRECT_CRON_PROMPT_RE` matches any user message starting with `[cron:...]`, including human-typed text mid-transcript. The code's own comment (lines 858-861) explicitly warns about this exact scenario but the code ignored it.

## User Impact

Users who happen to type `[cron:` as part of a question or prompt mid-conversation will find that `memory_search` cannot find any earlier content from that session. The archive content is silently dropped.

## Evidence

- **Root cause**: `session-files.ts:843` — wipe block fires on any matching message, not just the first
- **Fix**: guard wipe with `collected.length === 0` so only the very first message can trigger cron classification
- **Tests**: 38 passed (1 new test — `does not wipe archive content when [cron:...] appears mid-transcript`)
- **Existing cron archive tests**: all pass unchanged (37/37)
target.author: pandah97
